description_long: This house features a two-story main structure with a single-story extension, both topped with red gabled tile roofs. The exterior walls are finished in white stucco with tan trim around the windows, doors, and building corners, giving it a Mediterranean or Southern European appearance. The main facade has two large rectangular doors and four windows, with a row of small decorative protrusions beneath the upper windows. The extension has two small horizontal windows and a single door, while the main building includes a chimney on one end. The windows are simple and rectangular, and the overall design is functional and unadorned, with no visible balconies or porches. The house appears solidly built, likely with masonry or concrete walls, and is suitable for a suburban or rural neighborhood setting.
description: A two-story white stucco house with a red gabled tile roof, tan trim, and a single-story extension. Features include rectangular windows, two doors, a chimney, and simple, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two doors, four windows, tan trim, and decorative protrusions beneath the upper windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gabled roofs, chimney, and two small horizontal windows on the extension.
description_view_2: Rear view with four windows on the main building and two small windows on the extension, no doors visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the chimney, two windows on the main building, and the plain wall of the extension.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple rectangular doors with tan trim, no porch or steps, and minimal decorative elements.
